description: SSOT for L2 location sheet generation. Used by location_expand_sheets.py

global.quality_tags: cinematic still photo, highly detailed, sharp focus, natural color grading, consistent architecture
global.global_negative: different building, relocated landmarks, random city, style shift, people crowd, watermark, text, logo, deformed architecture, melting walls

master.wide.instruction: wide establishing shot of the location, no people or tiny distant silhouettes only, full readable architecture and landmarks
master.wide.style_lock: same place identity, fixed architecture, fixed palette, no character hero
master.wide.negative_extra: close-up portrait, hero character, fashion model, different city

angles.eye.instruction: same location, eye-level camera looking into the main space, same architecture and landmarks
angles.eye.style_lock: identical materials palette lighting logic scale

angles.reverse.instruction: same location, reverse camera angle looking back toward the opposite side, same place
angles.reverse.style_lock: identical architecture palette landmarks relative positions

angles.high.instruction: same location, high-angle near top-down readable layout of the set

angles.low.instruction: same location, low-angle dramatic camera from ground level, same architecture

angles.empty_stage.instruction: same location as empty stage for character insert, clear foreground midground, no people, space to place a person, readable background set
angles.empty_stage.style_lock: same architecture palette lighting, uncluttered foreground

lighting.day.instruction: same location establishing composition, daylight natural lighting, clear soft shadows

lighting.golden.instruction: same location, golden hour warm side light, same architecture

lighting.night.instruction: same location at night, practical lamps, coherent night grade, same architecture

landmarks.prop_a.instruction: close-up study of the primary signature landmark or prop of this location, material detail, same style

landmarks.prop_b.instruction: close-up study of a secondary landmark or texture of this location
